task: I found a wallet left behind in a hotel room and I want to handle it the right way without overcomplicating it. Can you help me draft a simple note for the front desk and a quick checklist for what to do next?

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a simple, plain version you can use.

## Quick note for front desk
**Left-behind wallet found in room [room number].**  
Found by housekeeping on [date/time]. Wallet has been turned in to front desk / held per hotel policy. Please log it as lost and found and contact guest if needed.

## Quick checklist
- [ ] Don’t go through the wallet
- [ ] Check the room number and time found
- [ ] Turn it in to the front desk right away
- [ ] Write down what it is and where it was found
- [ ] Follow hotel lost-and-found policy
- [ ] If the guest returns, send them to the front desk

## Simple reminder
**Do not open or sort through personal items. Turn it in and document it.**

If you want, I can make this even shorter so it fits on a small handoff card.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that’s fine, but make the note a little more like how I’d actually write it at work. Shorter. Put in room number, time, and where it was found. Something I can just hand over בלי messing with it. Also the checklist is good, but I’d want one line about not keeping it at my cart or in the linen room. That’s the kind of thing people forget.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a shorter, more work-like version:

## Front desk note
**Wallet found in room [room number] at [time], [exact location in room]. Turned in per policy.**

## Quick checklist
- [ ] Don’t look through it
- [ ] Don’t keep it at your cart or in the linen room
- [ ] Turn it in to front desk right away
- [ ] Write down room number, time, and where it was found
- [ ] Follow lost-and-found policy

**Do not hold onto personal items. Turn them in and document it.**

If you want, I can make it even more like a handwritten log note.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. Short and plain, just how I’d write it. The line about not leaving it at the cart or in the linen room is good too. That’s the part folks forget.
